stories.body: President Donald Trump said negotiators have “just made a great settlement” with Iran that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz and bar Tehran from ever acquiring a nuclear weapon, while Iranian foreign ministry spokesperson Esmail Baghaei acknowledged most of the memorandum’s text is agreed but insisted Washington is making new demands and that “nothing has been finalised,” leaving regional leaders skeptical even as oil prices fell on the prospect of a deal.
stories.headline: Trump touts imminent Iran peace deal as Tehran insists nothing is finalized

stories.body: US Central Command said American forces shot down four Iranian drones targeting shipping in the Strait of Hormuz and then hit Iranian surveillance radars at Goruk and Qeshm Island, as Washington pursues indirect talks over an interim ceasefire while President Trump warns Tehran retains roughly 22% of its missile arsenal and pressures Iran to reopen the strait under any deal.
stories.headline: US forces strike Iranian coastal radar sites after downing drones near Strait of Hormuz

stories.body: Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araqchi told Al Mayadeen that the war with the United States will not end without an accompanying ceasefire in Lebanon and an Israeli pullout from occupied territory, reiterating Tehran’s support for Hezbollah as cross-border attacks continue and warning that any interim US-Iran agreement must also address sanctions relief, Strait of Hormuz access and frozen oil revenues.
stories.headline: Iran ties Hezbollah ceasefire and Israeli withdrawal to any broader deal with Washington

stories.body: Kyiv officials reported multiple fires, including a nine-story apartment building in Podil and burning cars in Obolon, after Russia launched one of the heavy strikes President Volodymyr Zelenskiy had warned about, urging residents to heed air-raid alerts as Moscow threatens systematic attacks on the capital following a deadly strike in occupied Luhansk.
stories.headline: Russian missile barrage batters Kyiv as apartment blocks and cars burn

stories.body: An air leak in Russia’s Zvezda module doubled from one to two pounds of air per day, prompting NASA to direct five astronauts into their docked Crew Dragon safe havens for two hours before Roscosmos paused its repair attempts; both agencies say the situation is stable, though disagreements over using a saw to access the suspected crack highlighted ongoing diplomatic tension aboard the ISS, which has never been evacuated in its 27-year history.
stories.headline: NASA orders ISS crew to Dragon capsules during worsening air leak before reversing evacuation alert

stories.body: The US Supreme Court left in place an injunction finding Alabama’s nitrogen hypoxia protocol unconstitutionally cruel, forcing the state to abandon its planned execution of Jeffery Lee hours after the scheduled time and marking the ninth blocked attempt to use the new method, which corrections officials now say they will not replace with another approach immediately.
stories.headline: Supreme Court blocks Alabama from executing inmate with nitrogen gas

stories.body: After The New York Times published accounts from ex-girlfriends alleging Graham Platner physically abused them and knowingly wore a Nazi-style tattoo, Democratic strategists, donors and elected officials split over whether to push the frontrunner from Maine’s Senate primary, with some citing the party’s need to defeat GOP Sen. Susan Collins while others warn Platner’s scandals and sexting history threaten core values and November viability.
stories.headline: Democrats erupt over new allegations against Maine Senate nominee Graham Platner

stories.body: Rep. Nancy Mace was eliminated from South Carolina’s gubernatorial primary after alienating Donald Trump by aiding release of Jeffrey Epstein documents and facing a barrage of negative ads, allowing Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette and Attorney General Alan Wilson to advance while leaving Mace without a political landing spot as she prepares to leave Congress later this year.
stories.headline: Nancy Mace loses South Carolina GOP governor primary after defying Trump on Epstein files

stories.body: Appraisals by jeweler Ansorena and the Spanish Gemological Institute put the trove of rubies, sapphires, emeralds and small diamonds taken from former prime minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero’s office safe at about €1.3 million, prompting investigators in the Plus Ultra influence-peddling case to examine whether the pieces—said to be family heirlooms and travel gifts—were properly declared for tax purposes or linked to potential money laundering.
stories.headline: Zapatero’s seized jewels valued at €1.3 million as tax, money-laundering probes widen

stories.body: Former Socialist militant Leire Díez has filed appeals arguing Spain’s National Court unlawfully opened a secret, parallel case accusing her of orchestrating a plot to destabilize investigations involving the PSOE and government, claiming the new probe duplicates allegations she has faced in a Madrid court since July 2025 and violates the non bis in idem principle and her defense rights.
stories.headline: Leire Díez fights to annul National Court probe into alleged plot to derail PSOE-related cases

stories.body: Madrid judge Antonio Viejo granted the Civil Guard’s UCO access to bank records from 16 financial institutions dating back to 2014 for Alberto González Amador, partner of Madrid regional president Isabel Díaz Ayuso, to trace assets tied to alleged Quirón Group kickbacks, including the €500,000 purchase of Círculo de Belleza S.L., as investigators seek signs of disproportionate enrichment connected to suspected bribery.
stories.headline: Judge authorizes sweeping review of Ayuso partner’s bank accounts amid bribery probe

stories.body: Spain’s June CIS barometer puts the PSOE at 31.3% support, down 4.9 points from May after former prime minister Zapatero’s indictment and the Leire Díez case dominated headlines, while the PP climbs to 27.1%, narrowing the Socialist lead to 4.2 points even as Pedro Sánchez’s party still tops national voting intentions.
stories.headline: CIS poll shows PSOE plunging nearly five points after Zapatero indictment and Leire case

stories.body: Roughly 300 singers, including many of the 500 ejected from the Sagrada Família during Pope Leo XIV’s mass for carrying sheet music printed with estelades, gathered at the Passion façade to perform “Els Segadors,” “Virolai” and “La santa espina” while waving independence flags, snarling traffic on Carrer de Sardenya for 45 minutes and denouncing the earlier police action as intimidation.
stories.headline: Expelled choir stages protest outside Sagrada Família after police block ‘Els Segadors’ during papal mass

stories.body: Following a string of drug-related shootings—including a fatal attack on Carrer de Balmes—the Catalan Interior Department and Mossos d’Esquadra urged Madrid to raise prison terms for illegal firearm possession from the current one to two years up to five, and to triple penalties for large marijuana grows to nine years, arguing that carrying a gun in Spain “surt bastant gratis” and tougher laws would deter organized crime operating along the coast.
stories.headline: Fatal shooting on Barcelona’s Carrer de Balmes fuels fresh calls to toughen gun penalties

stories.body: From 3 July to 13 September, Trambesòs lines T4, T5 and T6 will be partially suspended near Plaça de les Glòries to realign track between Carrer de Badajoz and Carrer de Bilbao and double a single-track segment, with replacement buses, metro lines L1-L4, and Rodalies R1 recommended; Diagonal traffic will divert via Carrer de Tànger, several bus routes and bike lanes will shift, and residual closures will continue into autumn as work proceeds toward a spring 2027 completion.
stories.headline: Summer works will cut Tram Besòs lines T4, T5 and T6 around Glòries from July to mid-September

stories.body: A Mercasa auction seeking €25 million for the dilapidated Centre de la Vila shopping complex drew no bidders, leaving Vila Olímpica neighbors urging Barcelona City Hall and other administrations to intervene, coordinate needed repairs estimated at €3 million, and guard against speculators by exercising purchase rights so the site can be repurposed for community services after years of vacant shops and neglected maintenance.
stories.headline: Auction to sell Vila Olímpica’s moribund Centre de la Vila mall fails amid resident pressure
